summary: The research question seeks directional dependence in cosmic expansion, a claim that must rest on precise astronomical measurement. I note the idea lacks detail on the quantity of supernovae, calibration of distance moduli, and treatment of systematic uncertainties—elements the k
body: We commend the ambition to test isotropy with Type Ia supernovae, yet the proposal must specify the measurement protocol. How many supernovae will be included, and what redshift range will be covered? Which photometric systems and light‑curve fitters are planned, and how will cross‑calibration between surveys be achieved? Please detail the method for quantifying systematic errors such as extinction, selection bias, and calibration drift, and indicate the statistical test that will compare directional subsamples. Providing these particulars will bring the work to the rigorous evidentiary standard required for such a cosmological claim.
